goal: Empirically map unlabeled AppleSMC sensor keys to physical subsystems by correlating controlled stimuli against which keys respond.
method: For each stimulus: sample all numeric SMC keys during a per-rep idle pre-phase, during sustained load, and during cooldown. A key that rises specifically under one stimulus is hypothesized to sense that subsystem. Thermal crosstalk means labels are correlational, not ground truth.

constraints.smc_access: read-only (the reader binary has no write path)
constraints.loads: every load process is timeout-bounded; children killed on exit via trap
constraints.disk: writes a small self-deleting temp file, gated on >5GB free

stimuli.name: baseline
stimuli.hypothesis: Captures resting values and ambient/battery drift. Keys that move here are environmental, not load-driven.

stimuli.name: cpu_all
stimuli.exercises: all 14 cores at default QoS (P-cores + E-cores), busy integer loops
stimuli.hypothesis: P-core cluster temps/power should dominate: Tp*, TPD*, TCM*, PZC*, cluster power rails.

stimuli.name: cpu_e
stimuli.exercises: background-QoS busy loops (taskpolicy -b) biased onto efficiency cores
stimuli.hypothesis: Differentiates E-core sensors (Te*, separate cluster) from P-core sensors by comparison to cpu_all.

stimuli.name: memory
stimuli.exercises: streaming memory bandwidth via large-block dd zero->null
stimuli.hypothesis: DRAM / memory-controller sensors and memory power rails (Tm*, PMVR/PMVC) should respond more than under pure compute.

stimuli.name: disk
stimuli.exercises: repeated fsync'd writes to the internal SSD
stimuli.hypothesis: NAND / SSD-controller thermal sensors should respond; isolates storage from compute.

deferred.gpu: Needs a dedicated Metal compute stressor; skipped this run to keep it reliable and unattended.
deferred.ane: Neural Engine needs a CoreML model to load on demand; deferred.
deferred.per_core_isolation: macOS gives QoS hints, not hard core affinity, so distinguishing individual P-cores (TPD0 vs TPD1) is unreliable.
